I agree with option A because the proximal wall of a Class II cavity on a deciduous molar primarily follows the direction of enamel rods, which run horizontally from the DEJ to the occlusal surface—this preserves tooth integrity by aligning with natural enamel architecture. This alignment reduces risk of crack propagation and enhances long-term restoration success compared to divergent or converging designs that may compromise remaining structure. Final Answer: A.
